2014 - zungoli, italy voluntary work voluntary work at san nicola oratory in zungoli, as children entertainer 15/11/2021 - 24/11/2021 - online esa - online product assurance awareness training course 2021 eight different sessions organized by the esa academy team, where many different esa experts explained to the selected students the importance of product assurance in space business and the role of a pa engineer during the different phases of a space project. the lectures focused on all the different pa disciplines: - quality management and assurance - materials and processes - safety - software product assurance - dependability - radiation hardness assurance - eee components the knowledge learned during the training week has been tested with several exercises and a final evaluation test, which was crucial for the issue of the certificate. 10/09/2021 - 30/03/2022 - naples, italy internship at federico ii university of naples identification of the parent body/bodies for an unclassified space debris cloud and modeling of the fragmentation event (collision or explosion) which the cloud originated from. main steps: o a set of unclassified objects in the form of two-line element (tle) data is analyzed. o possible orbital intersections are searched in order to identify the objects which will appear in a small region of space at the same. o the objects are propagated backward to analyze their evolution in time searching for convergence of their osculating orbital elements. o these are then matched with a catalog of known objects to provide a guess of the possible operative or dismissed spacecraft involved in the explosion or collision. o all the previous activities are performed using a particular algorithm implemented in matlab and providing real-time simulations with gmat software. 01/05/2022 - current - naples, italy scolarship at federico ii university of naples actively involved in the current research of the aerospace systems team of the federico ii university. main activities are related to space surveillance and tracking. in particular, the attention is focused on different parent identification techniques and on the development of a new algorithm used as space fragments' analyzer. e education and training 09/2016 - 02/07/2021 melchior - micro-satellite explorer to a long-period comet in a heliocentric inner orbit melchior project is representative of phases 0/a of a space mission design with the aim of performing a fly-by to a long-period comet (lpc), in order to characterize its surface, shape, structure and chemical composition. the work demonstrated the feasibility of the mission by means of a single satellite with a mass lower than 100 kg and an envelope size of 1m x 1m x 1m, guaranteeing a probability of success larger than 90%. roles covered: - chief of mission analysis work package: 1. waiting phase * definition of the reference trajectory for the parking orbit (earth-moon l2 near rectilinear halo orbit with perilune radius of 5931 km) * development of the station keeping algorithm * design of the eventual eclipses avoidance maneuvers 2. heliocentric transfer * choice of the most favorable starting point * definition of the different strategies considered with different propulsion systems * desing of the transfer to the backup target 3. fly-by phase * evaluation of the geometry at the encounter (target-satellite-sun) 4. post-encounter phase * estimate of the distance from the earth for different cases - actively worked in telecommunication work package: * link budget analysis (downlink and uplink) * selection of the hardware components - actively worked in risks, reliability & costs work package: * definition of all the mission risks with their respective mitigation plan * costs estimation using small satellite cost model * evaluation of the system reliability for the different phases of the mission using weibull distribution e honours and awards 13/11/2021 finalist at the 7th mission idea contest 2021 for micro/nano-satellite utilization - japan aerospace exploration agency - jaxa
